description: JSON REST API Backend (Read-Only) — C++ HTTP server in src/ serving JSON at /api/v1/* on port 2009, backed by SQLite, also serving the React SPA

userStories.title: Modular source layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the src/ directory organized into modules so that code is maintainable and each layer has clear responsibilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory structure exists: src/util/, src/domain/, src/db/, src/net/, src/app/
Each module has its own CMakeLists.txt producing a static library (except app which produces the executable)
Top-level CMakeLists.txt includes all modules via add_subdirectory
Dependency graph is acyclic: util <- domain <- db <- net <- app
userStories.notes: Modules wired: util/domain/db/db/net/app each have CMakeLists.txt producing static libs (app = executable). Acyclic chain. Root CMakeLists uses add_subdirectory.

userStories.title: Utility module (string and time)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need string and time utilities so that the backend can convert between internal representations and JSON-friendly formats.
acceptanceCriteria: src/util/string_util.h/.cpp provides toUTF8(wstring) -> string and fromUTF8(string) -> wstring
src/util/time_util.h/.cpp provides formatTimeHMS(int seconds) -> string returning "HH:MM:SS" and parseTimeHMS(string) -> int parsing "HH:MM:SS" to seconds
Unit tests verify round-trip conversion for strings with Swedish characters (e.g., "IF Berget", "Bjornparken, Stockholm")
Unit tests verify formatTimeHMS(36000) == "10:00:00" and parseTimeHMS("10:02:00") == 36120
userStories.notes: Implemented string_util (toUTF8/fromUTF8 hand-rolled UTF-8 codec, no platform deps) and time_util (formatTimeHMS/parseTimeHMS). Unit tests in tests/util_test.cpp cover Swedish characters, round-trip, and invalid input.

userStories.title: Domain entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need C++ structs matching the frontend's TypeScript types so that serialization is straightforward.
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/entities.h defines structs: Competition, Club, Control, Course, Class, Runner, Team, SplitTime, Result, StartListEntry
Field names and types match src/ui/web/src/types/index.ts exactly (std::string for strings, int for numbers, std::vector<int> for arrays, std::optional for optional fields)
src/domain/entities.h is usable from all other modules
userStories.notes: Created src/domain/entities.h with structs Competition, Club, Control, Course, Class, Runner, Team, SplitTime, Result, StartListEntry matching TypeScript types exactly. std::optional for optional fields, std::vector<int> for arrays of IDs.

userStories.title: SQLite database layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database abstraction that creates tables, seeds test data, and provides read access for all entities.
acceptanceCriteria: src/db/database.h/.cpp provides a Database class that opens/creates a SQLite file
Database::createTables() creates tables for all entities (competitions, clubs, controls, courses, course_controls, classes, runners, teams, team_members, results, splits, start_list)
PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON and PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL are set on connection
Read methods exist: getAllClubs() -> vector<Club>, getClubById(int) -> optional<Club>, and equivalent for all entities
getAllCourses() joins course_controls to populate the controls vector
getAllTeams() joins team_members to populate the members vector
getAllResults() joins splits to populate the splits vector
sqlite3 is added to vcpkg.json and linked in CMake
Unit tests verify CRUD operations against an in-memory SQLite database (:memory:)
userStories.notes: Database class with SQLite3 (vcpkg), createTables for all entities, read methods with JOIN for courses/teams/results. Tests use :memory: DB.

userStories.title: Database seed data
userStories.description: As a user, I want the server to start with test data so that the frontend shows real content immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: When competitions table is empty, seed inserts data matching src/ui/web/src/mocks/db.ts exactly: 1 competition, 5 clubs, 7 controls, 5 courses (with course_controls), 5 classes, 6 runners, 5 teams (with team_members), 6 results (with splits), 6 start list entries
When the database already has data, seeding is skipped
Entity IDs in seed data match the mock IDs (e.g., club "IF Berget" has id=1)
userStories.notes: Implemented seedIfEmpty(Database&) in src/db/seed.h/.cpp. Seeds all entities matching db.ts mock data exactly (1 competition, 5 clubs, 7 controls, 5 courses with course_controls, 5 classes, 6 runners, 5 teams with team_members, 6 results with splits, 6 start list entries). Skips when data exists. Wrapped in transaction for atomicity.

userStories.title: HTTP server with CORS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP server that handles CORS and serves as the foundation for the API and SPA.
acceptanceCriteria: OPTIONS requests on /api/* return 204 with CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS and Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type)
cpp-httplib is added to vcpkg.json and linked in CMake
userStories.notes: HttpServer in src/net/http_server.h/.cpp wraps cpp-httplib 0.39.0. CORS via set_pre_routing_handler: all responses get Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *; OPTIONS /api/* returns 204 with Allow-Methods and Allow-Headers. Port configurable (default 2009). cpp-httplib added to vcpkg.json, linked as httplib::httplib.

userStories.title: JSON API — Clubs endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real club data on the Clubs page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/clubs returns JSON array of all clubs e.g. [{"id":1,"name":"IF Berget","country":"SE"}, ...]
GET /api/v1/clubs/{id} returns single club object or 404
Response body is a plain array/object (no {"data":...} envelope) — matching the MSW mock format
nlohmann-json is added to vcpkg.json and linked in CMake
Integration test starts server on a test port, performs HTTP GET, and verifies JSON structure
userStories.notes: Implemented registerClubsRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp using nlohmann-json 3.12.0. GET /api/v1/clubs returns plain JSON array; GET /api/v1/clubs/{id} returns single object or 404 with {message,status} shape. Optional country field omitted when absent. Integration tests via httplib::Client against server on port 18007 — 4 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Controls endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real control data on the Controls page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/controls returns JSON array e.g. [{"id":1,"code":101,"description":"Fork junction","type":"normal"}, ...]
Response Content-Type is application/json with no envelope wrapper
userStories.notes: registerControlsRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp. GET /api/v1/controls returns plain JSON array; GET /api/v1/controls/{id} returns single object or 404. Optional description/type fields omitted when absent. Integration tests via httplib::Client on port 18008 — 4 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Courses endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real course data on the Courses page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/courses/{id} returns single course with controls array, or 404
controls field is an ordered array of control IDs matching the course's control sequence
userStories.notes: registerCoursesRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp. GET /api/v1/courses returns plain JSON array; GET /api/v1/courses/{id} returns single course with controls array or 404. Optional length field omitted when absent. nlohmann-json serializes vector<int> controls directly. Integration tests via httplib::Client on port 18009 — 4 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Classes endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real class data on the Classes page.
userStories.notes: registerClassesRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp. GET /api/v1/classes returns plain JSON array; GET /api/v1/classes/{id} returns single object or 404. Optional courseId/startMethod fields omitted when absent. Integration tests via httplib::Client on port 18010 — 4 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Runners endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real runner data on the Runners page.
acceptanceCriteria: Optional fields (clubId, classId, startTime, cardNumber, status) are included only when present (not serialized as null)
userStories.notes: registerRunnersRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp. GET /api/v1/runners returns plain JSON array; GET /api/v1/runners/{id} returns single object or 404. All optional fields (clubId, classId, startTime, cardNumber, status) omitted when absent. runnerToJson helper avoids duplication. Integration tests via httplib::Client on port 18011 — 4 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Teams endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real team data on the Teams page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/teams/{id} returns single team with members array, or 404
userStories.notes: registerTeamsRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp. GET /api/v1/teams returns plain JSON array; GET /api/v1/teams/{id} returns single team with members array or 404. Optional clubId/classId fields omitted when absent. members (vector<int>) serializes directly to JSON array. Integration tests via httplib::Client on port 18012 — 4 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Competition endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real competition info on the Competition page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/competitions returns the competition object e.g. {"id":1,"name":"Spring Cup 2026","date":"2026-05-15","organizer":"IF Berget","location":"Bjornparken, Stockholm","description":"Annual spring orienteering competition"}
Endpoint returns a singleton object, not an array
userStories.notes: registerCompetitionsRoutes in src/net/api.h/.cpp. GET /api/v1/competitions returns singleton competition object (not array) — takes first result from getAllCompetitions(). All required fields (id, name, date, organizer, location) always included; optional description omitted when absent. Returns 404 if no competition in DB. Integration tests via httplib::Client on port 18013 — 3 tests passing.

userStories.title: JSON API — Results endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real results on the Results page.
acceptanceCriteria: Results with no position/totalTime (e.g., DNS/DNF) omit those fields or set them to null
splits is an array of {controlId, time} objects

userStories.title: JSON API — Start list endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to see real start list data on the Start List page.

userStories.title: XML export endpoints (IOF 3.0)
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want to export results and start lists as IOF XML.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/results/export/xml returns IOF 3.0 ResultList XML with Content-Type: application/xml
GET /api/v1/startlist/export/xml returns IOF 3.0 StartList XML with Content-Type: application/xml
Integration test verifies Content-Type and namespace presence

userStories.title: SPA static file serving with fallback
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open http://localhost:2009 in my browser and see the full React application.
acceptanceCriteria: The server serves static files from a configurable directory (default: src/ui/web/dist/)
GET /assets/foo.js serves the corresponding file with the correct Content-Type
Any GET request that does not match /api/* and does not match a static file returns index.html (SPA fallback)

userStories.title: Application entry point
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run a single binary that starts the server with a seeded database.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup: opens/creates meos.sqlite, runs createTables(), runs seedIfEmpty(), registers all API routes, starts HTTP server on port 2009
Server logs startup message to stdout: 'MeOS server listening on http://localhost:2009'
cmake --preset default && cmake --build build produces a working meos binary
Running ./build/meos starts the server and curl http://localhost:2009/api/v1/clubs returns valid JSON

userStories.title: Frontend backend toggle (VITE_USE_BACKEND)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to easily switch between MSW mocks and the real backend during development.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/web/src/main.tsx checks for VITE_USE_BACKEND env variable
When VITE_USE_BACKEND is set (any truthy value), MSW worker is NOT started — requests go to the real backend via the Vite proxy
When VITE_USE_BACKEND is not set (default), MSW starts as before — no behavior change for frontend-only development
npm test still passes (tests always use MSW regardless of env var)
